key_insight: A hybrid material point method can accurately capture bending of highly flexible slender structures by coupling cross-sectional deformation with lengthwise bending and contact.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical simulation tool for designing soft robots, deployable structures, tubes, and tape springs where large deformation and contact are critical.
method: Hybrid-MPM combines Lagrangian finite elements with Eulerian finite differences.
Simulates experimental three-point bending tests with complex contact mechanics.
Applied to elastic tubes and tape springs as canonical bending cases.
evidence_signal: Simulation results were validated against desktop experiments and classical theory, showing excellent agreement.
watch_item: The framework still requires careful implementation of contact handling and material/contact parameter calibration for reliable predictive use.
paper_title: Numerical and experimental framework for bending elasticity of highly flexible slender structures
